target.title: feat: add experimental --provider wasi delegated-run provider (wazero primary, wasmtime opt-in)
target.body:
feat: add experimental --provider wasi delegated-run provider (wazero primary, wasmtime opt-in)

**Why (core value, per strong rationale + ultrathink + subagent analysis)**
- Fits architecture perfectly: Delegated providers (e2b, modal, islo, tensorlake, etc.) already exist for exactly this — no SSH lease, just “sync + run + stream”. WASI slots in as lightweight, zero-OS sandbox.
- Capsule synergy: Capsules already capture failures portably. WASI replay = true zero-deps (run .wasm anywhere with wasmtime/wazero). Ultimate reproducibility for AI agents/CI.
- Peter’s workflow: top contributor; his agent runs already mix crabbox + Wasm. This extends it natively.
- Security/portability win: Capability-based isolation stricter than Firecracker/containers. Perfect for untrusted code, AI agents. (See wasi.dev: "secure standard interface ... no ambient authority" via explicit capabilities/preopens.)
- Future-proof: WASI 0.2/components exploding (wasi.dev + WebAssembly/WASI specs). Makes crabbox control-plane for Wasm sandboxes too.
- Low effort, high upside for capsules + agents. "Peter would merge it in a day."

**What (minimal viable, per the pasted case)**
- New internal/providers/wasi (provider + core aliases `wasm`/`wazero` + backend implementing the delegated contract).
- Primary: wazero v1.12.0 (pure-Go embed, NewRuntime + wasi_snapshot_preview1.MustInstantiate + WithDirMount only /work). Opt-in: wasmtime CLI (`--wasi-runtime wasmtime`).
- CLI surface: `crabbox run --provider wasi -- ./my.wasm arg1` (or `echo`/`true` fast-path, builtins ls/cat on guest tree, absolute .wasm paths supported).
- Sync: same manifest + copy to isolated guest root (preopen as /work); respects --no-sync, Delete, excludes. Fast bypass for no-FS builtins.
- Full contract: ProviderSpec with DelegatedRun | ArchiveSync | RunSession | RunProof; RunResult always includes Session; warmup/status/stop/doctor/list all wired.
- Config: wasi: {runtime, workdir, guestBaseDir}; flags --wasi-*; env CRABBOX_WASI_*.
- Capsules: .wasm + manifest captured; replayable with zero host OS deps.
- Tests: on-the-fly GOOS=wasip1 wasi compile + table tests (bypass, real module, args, exit, Session, -race).
- Integration: all providers registration, cli, doctor --all, live-smoke.sh (wasi_smoke), matrices, source-map, docs.
- Honest docs: experimental/specialized; "When it shines" (Wasm-first, capsules, agents, untrusted); "disappoints" (80% legacy suites need full POSIX); shines for "Local loop, remote box" promise via same interface.

**Online sources checked (user: "wasi!!!!! check online" + github + wasi.dev)**
- https://github.com/WebAssembly/WASI : spec, Preview 1 (wasi_snapshot_preview1 / witx for compat), 0.2 (WIT/Component Model).
- https://wasi.dev/ : "The WebAssembly System Interface (WASI) is a group of standards-track API specifications for software compiled to the W3C WebAssembly (Wasm) standard. WASI is designed to provide a secure standard interface for applications that can be compiled to Wasm from any language, and that may run anywhere..."; runtimes list includes wazero + wasmtime; emphasis on modules vs components; "For runtime developers": host implements/embeds and controls capabilities granted (e.g. which dirs to preopen) — exactly matches our /work preopen + deny-default.
- Backend comment + docs/providers/wasi.md updated with quotes + alignment (capability model, preopen, preview1/2, listed runtimes).

**Verification (all gates + manual to finish line)**
- `go build -trimpath -o bin/crabbox ./cmd/crabbox`
- `go test -race ./internal/providers/wasi/...`
- `./scripts/check-docs.sh` (command surface + 143 md links + site build)
- Manual:
  - `bin/crabbox doctor --provider wasi --json`
  - `bin/crabbox run --provider wasi -- echo '...' ` (bypass, sync_skipped)
  - Real: compile wasip1 .wasm on fly + `bin/crabbox run --provider wasi -- /tmp/hello.wasm arg1 arg2` (absolute path, args passed, preopen /work, exit 0)
  - wasmtime fallback path (graceful when not in $PATH)
  - live-smoke patterns
- jj on `feat/wasi`: clean history, conventional commits.
- No core pollution: all behind Spec features + provider interface.

**Risks / why not (honest, per analysis)**
- Niche adoption today: most suites not Wasm-native (POSIX limits: no apt, limited FS/threads/sockets in P1).
- 5-20% CPU overhead possible; wasmtime optional.
- Maintenance tracks fast-evolving WASI (0.3 proposals etc.).
- Positioned **experimental** (flag/docs): protects flagship "remote box" + "many providers one loop" promises while shipping specialized value.

See docs/providers/wasi.md (full "when it shines / disappoints", examples, capsule story, config) and internal/providers/wasi/backend.go .

Closes the deep "should we add WASI?" (ditch once, then "consider but do what's right" + user strong case + "finish it and go to the finish line!!!!!").

**PR created** via jj + gh from the contributor workspace (remotes: origin=zozo123/crabbox fork, upstream=openclaw). See FINISH_LINE_PR.md in the tree for the exact commands used.

Ready for review (Peter ideal). Use for capsules, agent sandboxes, Wasm-first CI.
target.author: zozo123
